# Franclloyd D. Dagdag — Full-Stack Web Developer

> Portfolio of Franclloyd D. Dagdag, a full-stack web developer from the Philippines
> specializing in scalable system architecture, complex problem solving, and precision
> engineering. Builds end-to-end products across React, Next.js, Node.js, Python, and
> Laravel, with a focus on performance, maintainability, and clean system design.

Franclloyd has shipped government-scale platforms (Internal Audit Management System,
Procurement Management System, blockchain-backed financial audit) and rebuilt client
web presences for speed and SEO. B.S. Information Technology, Caraga State University
(magna cum laude, 2023).

## Pages

- [Home / Portfolio](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/): Overview, skills, experience, featured projects, and contact.
- [Blog — Case Studies](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog): Index of in-depth case studies on real client work.
- [Two Custom Sites in Three Days](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/two-sites-three-days): Two complete, custom React websites built for one client (Kiona) in 72 hours — a premium Kangen water wellness brand (HydraWellnessLux) and a high-ticket sales coaching platform (LimitlessKBL). Two distinct brand identities on a fixed three-day deadline, both shipped on time; a look at the reusable component system and workflow that makes fast, fully custom delivery possible without cutting corners.
- [Acro Refrigeration Case Study](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/acro-refrigeration): Migrating a 50+ year Australian refrigeration company from a slow WordPress site to a Next.js + Supabase platform with a custom CMS — 100+ blog posts migrated, ~10x faster load times, and zero SEO regression.
- [Acro Refrigeration — 90 Days Later](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/acro-refrigeration-90-days): A data-driven follow-up 90 days after the Acro Refrigeration rebuild — visitors up 110% month over month, 91.5K Google search impressions, a PageSpeed score holding at 95 performance / 100 SEO, and early AI-assistant discovery (chatgpt.com referrals, a 3/3 Agentic Browsing score).
- [HVACR Group — One Client, Four Websites](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/hvacr-group): After the Acro Refrigeration rebuild, the same Australian group (HVACR Group) returned to redesign three more of their brands — Shelair, HVACR Group, and Koolacube — off WordPress onto one shared Next.js + Supabase stack. Every site scores 90+ performance and 100 SEO; Koolacube's performance rose from 44 to 93.
- [WordPress vs. Hardcoded](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/wordpress-vs-hardcoded): A field-notes breakdown contrasting WordPress with hand-built, hardcoded websites — where custom code gains its edge in speed, security, SEO, and ownership, and how WordPress becomes a disadvantage over the long run.
- [wp2shell — Pre-Auth RCE in WordPress Core](https://www.daggerbuilds.com/blog/wp2shell-wordpress-core-rce): Analysis of wp2shell (CVE-2026-60137 + CVE-2026-63030), a pre-authentication remote code execution chain in WordPress core disclosed July 17, 2026, affecting versions 6.9.x and 7.0.x with no plugins required. Covers how to check exposure, patch to 6.9.5 / 7.0.2, check a site for compromise, and why core-level flaws make every install vulnerable at the same instant.

## Skills

- Frontend: React, Next.js, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, Flutter
- Backend: Node.js, Python, Laravel
- Data: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Firebase, Supabase
- Other: Web3, Docker, WordPress, SEO
